hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
Here are the public test cases for feature9_server_status_metrics:
{
  "description": "Produce service health and transfer metrics from synchronized server state, including connectivity booleans, DHT node count, transfer counters, version, and server labels.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "operation": "status_metrics",
        "version": "1.2.3",
        "server_state": {
          "connection_status": "connected"
        }
      },
      "expected_output": "metric_count=8
metric[0].name=qbittorrent_up
metric[0].type=gauge
metric[0].value=True
metric[0].labels={"server":"localhost:8080/qbt/","version":"1.2.3"}
metric[0].help=Whether the qBittorrent server is answering requests from this exporter. A `version` label with the server version is added.
metric[1].name=qbittorrent_connected
metric[1].type=gauge
metric[1].value=True
metric[1].labels={"server":"localhost:8080/qbt/"}
metric[1].help=Whether the qBittorrent server is connected to the Bittorrent network.
metric[2].name=qbittorrent_firewalled
metric[2].type=gauge
metric[2].value=False
metric[2].labels={"server":"localhost:8080/qbt/"}
metric[2].help=Whether the qBittorrent server is connected to the Bittorrent network but is behind a firewall.
metric[3].name=qbittorrent_dht_nodes
metric[3].type=gauge
metric[3].value=0
metric[3].labels={"server":"localhost:8080/qbt/"}
metric[3].help=Number of DHT nodes connected to.
metric[4].name=qbittorrent_dl_info_data
metric[4].type=counter
metric[4].value=0
metric[4].labels={"server":"localhost:8080/qbt/"}
metric[4].help=Data downloaded since the server started, in bytes.
metric[5].name=qbittorrent_up_info_data
metric[5].type=counter
metric[5].value=0
metric[5].labels={"server":"localhost:8080/qbt/"}
metric[5].help=Data uploaded since the server started, in bytes.
metric[6].name=qbittorrent_alltime_dl
metric[6].type=counter
metric[6].value=0
metric[6].labels={"server":"localhost:8080/qbt/"}
metric[6].help=Total historical data downloaded, in bytes.
metric[7].name=qbittorrent_alltime_ul
metric[7].type=counter
metric[7].value=0
metric[7].labels={"server":"localhost:8080/qbt/"}
metric[7].help=Total historical data uploaded, in bytes.
"
    }
  ]
}

context_pointers.vague_reference: fill in the state and category placeholders from the current selection context
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The regex expects literal string interpolation of the BufferedReader state variable and the selected Category ID string.

context_pointers.vague_reference: match the version derivation pattern used in the first line of output
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The output string must contain a key-value pair 'version=<version_string>' where version_string is the current Bittorrent application version.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_endpoint_resolution.json.description: Build the monitored service endpoint from host, port, secure-transport flag, and optional URL base path.

feature2_metric_record_defaults.json.description: Represent a single metric record with its name and value while applying default labels, help text, and gauge type when optional metadata is omitted.

feature3_prometheus_family_rendering.jso.description: Convert a metric record into the corresponding Prometheus gauge or counter family with documentation, labels, sample name, and sample value preserved.

cases.expected_output:
family_type=gauge
family_name=test_gauge
documentation=Test Gauge
sample_name=test_gauge
sample_labels={"label1":"value1","server":"localhost:8080"}
sample_value=10

feature4_category_catalog_fetch.json.description: Return the category catalog from the remote service as ordinary JSON objects, and return an empty catalog when the category source cannot be read.

feature5_torrent_list_fetch.json.description: Return the torrent list from the remote service as ordinary JSON objects, and return an empty list when the torrent source cannot be read.

feature6_state_filtering.json.description: Select only torrents whose state exactly matches the requested torrent state; unmatched states produce an empty list.

feature7_category_filtering.json.description: Select only torrents whose category matches the requested category, treating a blank torrent category as Uncategorized when that category is requested.

feature8_torrent_count_metric.json.description: Create a gauge metric that reports the number of torrents for a specific state and category, preserving both values as labels along with the monitored server label.

cases.expected_output:
metric_name=qbittorrent_torrents_count
metric_type=gauge
value=10
labels={"category":"movies","server":"localhost:8080/qbt/","status":"downloading"}
help=Number of torrents in status downloading under category movies

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'endpoint' and config contains host, port, optional url_base
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain exactly two lines: 'server={host}:{port}{url_base}' and 'endpoint={protocol}://{host}:{port}{url_base}'
The URL base string must be empty; do not append slashes or separators when base is absent.

_constraints.input_condition: config.ssl is false and config.port is not 443
_constraints.output_rule: The generated endpoint protocol must be 'http'.

_constraints.input_condition: config.ssl is true OR config.port is '443'
_constraints.output_rule: The generated endpoint protocol must be 'https'.
Must output exactly 5 lines separated by newlines. Label key 'labels' must be within curly braces (e.g., '{}').
Line 2 must be 'metric_type=gauge'.

_constraints.input_condition: labels are not provided in input
_constraints.output_rule: Line 4 must be 'labels={}'.

_constraints.input_condition: help is not provided in input
_constraints.output_rule: Line 5 must be 'help=' (empty string).
Line 5 must be 'sample_name=test_gauge' where test_gauge matches input metric name. No underscores added.
Line 5 must be 'sample_name=test_name_total' where test_name matches input metric name and 'total' is appended with underscore.
Output must contain separated lines for 'family_type', 'family_name', 'documentation', 'sample_name', 'sample_labels', 'sample_value' prefixed correctly.
Line for sample_labels must contain '{}'.

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'category_catalog' and input.categories is a non-empty object
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be a single line starting with 'categories=...' containing a serialized JSON representation of the input categories object.

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'category_catalog' and input.categories is null or empty
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be a single line 'categories={}'.

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'torrent_list' and input.torrents is a non-empty list
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be a single line starting with 'torrents=[...]'.

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'torrent_list' and input.torrents is null or empty
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be a single line 'torrents=[]'.

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'select_by_state' and input.torrents list is provided
_constraints.output_rule: Filter torrents where item.state exactly equals input.state. Return matching list prefixed with 'torrents='. Preserve original order.

_constraints.input_condition: Input state matches none of the torrent items
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be 'torrents=[]'.

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'select_by_category' and requested category is not 'Uncategorized'
_constraints.output_rule: Filter torrents where item.category exactly equals input.category.

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'select_by_category' and requested category IS 'Uncategorized'
_constraints.output_rule: Filter torrents where item.category IS 'Uncategorized' OR item.category is an empty string (''). Include both in the result.

_constraints.input_condition: Input category matches none of the torrents
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be 'torrents=[]'.
Output must contain 5 lines. First line 'metric_name' must be '{prefix}_torrents_count'.
Line 5 'help' must be 'Number of torrents in status {state} under category {category}'.
Line 4 'labels' must contain keys 'category', 'server', and 'status' with corresponding values.
Output must start with line 'metric_count=8'.
Line for 'qbittorrent_up' value must be 'True' (capital T).
Line for 'qbittorrent_firewalled' value must be 'True'. Other connection states imply False.
Line for 'qbittorrent_connected' value must be 'True'.

_constraints.input_condition: operation is 'status_metrics' and input.server_state.connection_status is 'disconnected' or 'dht_down'
_constraints.output_rule: Line for 'qbittorrent_connected' value must be 'False'.
Metric names must contain 'qbittorrent_' prefix followed by specific metric identifier.
Numeric fields (dht_nodes, dl_bytes, ul_bytes) must default to 0 if not present in input.
The 'qbittorrent_up' metric value line must include labels: 'server' (derived from C001) and 'version' (from input).
All other metrics (connected, firewalled, dht, bytes) must include a 'server' label but no 'version' label.
All boolean metrics (up, connected, firewalled) must be type 'gauge'.
Byte counter metrics (dl_info, up_info, alltime_dl, alltime_ul) must be type 'counter'.
